metadata.name: Core Audit Rules
metadata.description: Basic SEO and agent-readiness checks for any website.

SEO-01.name: Single H1 Per Page
SEO-01.description: Each page must have exactly one H1 tag. Multiple H1s confuse AI agents and search engines about the primary topic. Zero H1s make the page untitled for agents.

fix.instruction: Ensure every page has exactly one <h1> tag describing the primary topic. If multiple H1s exist, downgrade extras to <h2>. If zero exist, add one at the top of <body>.
fix.codeSnippet: Primary Topic of This Page

SEO-02.name: Title Tag Presence and Length
SEO-02.description: Every page must have a <title> tag between 30 and 60 characters. Missing titles make pages untitled in search results and agent context. Too long gets truncated.

fix.instruction: Add a <title> tag inside <head> with 30-60 characters. Include primary keyword and brand name.

AG-01.name: HTML Lang Attribute
AG-01.description: The <html> tag must have a lang attribute. AI agents use this to determine language for summarization, translation, and content processing. Missing lang causes agent misinterpretation.

fix.instruction: Add lang attribute to the <html> tag using ISO 639-1 language code.
fix.codeSnippet:
<html lang="en">
<!-- or lang="bn" for Bangla, lang="zh" for Chinese -->

SEO-03.name: Meta Description Presence
SEO-03.description: Each page should have a meta description between 120 and 160 characters. While not a ranking factor, it controls search snippet text and agent summary context.

fix.instruction: Add a meta description summarizing the page content in 120-160 characters.
fix.codeSnippet: <meta name="description" content="This page covers backend engineering with NestJS, PostgreSQL, and scalable API design for high-traffic systems.">

examples.input: <meta name="description" content="A comprehensive guide to building scalable backends with NestJS and PostgreSQL for modern applications.">

SEO-04.name: Canonical Link Tag
SEO-04.description: Each page should have a canonical URL to prevent duplicate content issues. AI agents use canonicals to identify the authoritative version of content.

fix.instruction: Add a canonical link tag pointing to the preferred version of this URL.

OG-01.name: Open Graph Title
OG-01.description: og:title gives AI agents and social scrapers the canonical page title for cards and summaries. Missing it falls back to <title>, which may be truncated or wrong in agent context.

fix.instruction: Add an og:title meta tag with the page's share title (40-60 chars).

OG-02.name: Open Graph Description
OG-02.description: og:description supplies the summary text agents and social cards use. Without it, agents infer from page body, often producing weak or off-topic summaries.

fix.instruction: Add an og:description meta tag (120-200 chars) summarizing the page.
fix.codeSnippet: <meta property="og:description" content="Shop the durable, eco-friendly Widget with free shipping and a 2-year warranty.">

OG-03.name: Open Graph Image
OG-03.description: og:image is the visual agents and social platforms pull for cards. Missing it means no visual signal in agent summaries and zero share-preview CTR.

fix.instruction: Add an og:image meta tag pointing to a 1200x630 (or larger) share image URL.

OG-04.name: Open Graph Type
OG-04.description: og:type tells agents the semantic nature of the page (product, article, website). Agents use it to route the page into the correct understanding/extraction path.

JSONLD-01.name: JSON-LD Structured Data Present
JSONLD-01.description: Structured data (schema.org JSON-LD) is the primary signal AI agents (ChatGPT, Perplexity, Gemini, Claude) read to extract facts, products, and entities. A page with no JSON-LD is nearly invisible to agentic retrieval.

fix.instruction: Add a <script type="application/ld+json"> block with schema.org markup describing the page entity.

JSONLD-02.name: Product Schema Validity
JSONLD-02.description: When a Product schema is present, agents expect name, image, and a populated offers.price. Missing or mistyped fields break product cards in agent answers and shopping integrations.

fix.instruction: Ensure the Product JSON-LD includes name (string), image (absolute URL), offers.price (number), and sku (string). Set @context to https://schema.org.

JSONLD-03.name: Organization Schema Validity
JSONLD-03.description: Organization schema tells agents who publishes the site, enabling entity linking and brand mentions in agent answers. name and logo are the minimum agents use.

fix.instruction: Add Organization JSON-LD with name (string) and logo (absolute URL).
